author: @baraBober
text: Quality over quantity. Look at any successful channel. Some people post once a month and get millions of views

author: @paprjam
text: Now you tell us, you must have gotten some pretty bad videos. Hope they weren't mine. I think your videos show the right amount of info, some people show every solder joint and inserting components, not to mention talk too much or play annoying music.

author: @beardedbonsai
text: Thank you for more good advice.

author: @mauricewatts2380
text: It occurred to me that this was how informative television programmes were produced years ago. Such as gardening and DIY  programmes. In a 30 min slot they probably concentrated on only 2 or 3 topics and stayed focused on them. I suspect  that changes in technology have made it possible to jump all over time and place.

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: THE MOST VIEWS ARE GOTTEN BY VIDEOS THAT ARE FROM 7 TO 12 MIN.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: try making a boring vid that is that long - it won't help you get views - the most views are got by videos that are interesting and entertaining

replies.author: @TechKnowCSS
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering AGREED, BUT ARE FROM 7 TO 12 MIN.

author: @gregceth443
text: Sorry mate, 1.5 times and I clicked off five minutes in, the bar is getting higher each day.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no worries mate

author: @nelsonphillips
text: A good splash screen. I need to work on my prosody... speaking fast works to but is hard

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure

author: @lylecoglianese1645
text: Excellent advice!!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Glad you think so!

author: @ladamurni
text: Thank you Robert, it was very usefull.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Glad to hear it!

author: @dexterconnolly3696
text: Your enthusiasm is what got me hooked. Then the science. There's a lot of fake videos out there that might get a few views....you have over 1300 videos now and every single one is gold. Thankyou rob

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate and I greatly appreciate you saying that

author: @rayg436
text:
Great job 
I really appreciate you helping all of your viewers. Your channel is one I look forward to watching every day. Cheers mate.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: wow - thank you mate

author: @benniedonald
text: That's why I can't make videos. My ADHD  divides my attention into several categories. Plus my eyes wander around and roll with my thoughts. I have learned to finish my projects on my own. Then if I am pleased with the results. Just film those results. Otherwise I am a mishmash of incoherent babbling. LOL 😆 🤣

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - bless your heart mate and cheers

author: @thequixotryworkshop2424
text: Thanks Rob! Great tips worth following…

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I'm glad you found it useful mate

author: @kemet432
text: 😂Awesome idea/information👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Glad you liked it

author: @digitaldreamer5481
text: Let’s see…one idea, the depth of the video, the three steps of introduction, entertain beginning to end and the length of the video directly to the topic of the subject or video. I think that is why I enjoyed your last video of the wind wall so much and looking forward for the next when you add the motors and the testing of the overall project. Thank you very much! Mahalo

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: spot on mate and thank you very much for saying that

author: @____________________________.x
text: I've often wondered why you chose that intro music? I find it jarring to listen to and always skip the first minute

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yep - it's just the centre of the distribution mate - I am glad you skip it tbh lol

replies.author: @lootube9290
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering sorry Rob, I'm really not keen on it either, especially if I've left my volume up, that vibrato at the end has a queasy effect on me

author: @24hrninja
text: cheers

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @rachelramos984
text: But I love this ❤️❤️ 😀🤘🏼

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Thanks Rob, I need to get back into it, have been tied up lately with my son breaking his elbow and me having to do everything for him. Hopefully will be back next weekend.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: here's hoping mate

author: @mikebond6328
text: I’ve been thinking about giving it a go. I very much appreciate your advice. I actually hadn’t put much thought into how to go about making a video. I was kind of planning on just turning on the camera and letting it go. I realize now that would be a mistake. I, like many people I’m sure, underestimated the thought and effort that goes into it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it does take a bit of effort mate - but it is well worth it I think - the sharing of ideas and the community to talk to - it's pretty awesome

replies.author: @JK360noscope
replies.text:
That wouldn't be a mistake. It'd be a great success! You made your first video!

Remember what videos consisted of back in 2005/2006? It consisted of a guy turning on a camera, showing up to the zoo, and filming some elephants.

No script, no ridiculous click-bait thumbnail where everyone has malformed faces for additional traffic, no problem!

You got this Mike! Share what you know with the world!

author: @scantrain5007
text:
Dear Rob, 
thanks to bring it on the spot! You made clear, what was before to me only 'feeled' - and not published my own test-videos because I did the mistakes as you said: Too many subjects, confusing...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: do try again mate - it would be great to see your vids

text: Awesome! 👏
